sourceMovie.title: Saturday Night Fever
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Saturday Night Fever (1977) as a music theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The music and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Step Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Saturday Night Fever clicked for you, Step Up (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in music and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Saturday Night Fever?
faq.answer: Try Step Up, Step Up All In, Footloose—each shares DNA with Saturday Night Fever while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Saturday Night Fever?
faq.answer: Saturday Night Fever (1977) is best described as Music, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Saturday Night Fever part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Tony Manero Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
